title: HW1 Q5 — Solve $T(n) = 2T(n/2) + 1$
statement: Consider $T(n) = 2T(n/2) + 1$ with $T(1) = 1$. By repeatedly substituting, calculate $T(n)$ up to big-$\Theta$.

steps.title: Stop at the base case.
steps.body:
$n / 2^i = 1$ means $i = \log_2 n$. Plug in:

$$T(n) = 2^{\log_2 n} \cdot T(1) + (2^{\log_2 n - 1} + \cdots + 4 + 2 + 1) = n + \left(\tfrac{n}{2} + \tfrac{n}{4} + \cdots + 2 + 1\right).$$

tips.label: Log identity
tips.content: $2^{\log_2 n} = n$. Comes from $a^{\log_a x} = x$.

steps.title: Sum the finite geometric.
steps.body:
$$T(n) = n \left(1 + \tfrac{1}{2} + \tfrac{1}{4} + \cdots + \tfrac{1}{2^{\log_2 n}}\right).$$

The sum is at most $\sum_{i=0}^{\infty} (1/2)^i = \dfrac{1}{1 - 1/2} = 2$. So $T(n) \le 2n$.

Lower bound: $T(n) \ge n$ from the first term.

Therefore $T(n) = \Theta(n)$.

steps.title: Recursion-tree view (optional cross-check).
steps.body:
Level $i$: $2^i$ subproblems of size $n/2^i$, non-recursive work per subproblem $= 1$. Level work $= 2^i$. Increasing geometric!

Height $= \log_2 n$. Total across levels: $\sum_{i=0}^{\log_2 n - 1} 2^i = 2^{\log_2 n} - 1 = n - 1$.

Leaves: $2^{\log_2 n} = n$, each cost $1$. Total at leaves: $n$.

Grand total: $(n - 1) + n = 2n - 1 = \Theta(n)$. ✓
